EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A line of strong to severe storms ahead of a cold front brought damaging wind gusts and a few tornadoes to the tri-state area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tornado touched down in Walton County just west of Kings Lake Road and traveled east through a neighborhood along and just north of Paradise Island Drive. The tornado then presumably crossed the northern portion of Kings Lake causing more tree damage to the east of the lake. Along most of the path, EF-0 damage was observed. However, near the beginning of the patch two large trees were uprooted and rated EF-1. Near the end of the path, the tornado caused significant removal of the roofing material of a single family home. The porch was also badly damaged. Winds here were estimated at 95 mph, the highest found in this survey. The tornado then lifted quickly as there was no damage east of U.S 331 which is a swampy inaccessible area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A rare winter storm brought snowfall to the tri-state area.  In general, 1-2 inches of wet snow fell across portions of southeast Alabama and the adjacent Florida panhandle and southwest Georgia. The highest total of 3 inches was reported near Smithville in Lee County, GA.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Strong northerly post-frontal winds in the wake of a strong cold front advecting a record cold air mass into the region damage a mobile home near Nobleton in Sumter County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sumter County EM reported a carport collapse at a mobile home due to gusty winds. No injuries were reported.
